notice: MOCKED API RESPONSE SHAPED AFTER REAL SOURCES. Production source: Semantic Scholar Graph API, OpenAlex Works API, arXiv, and publisher metadata.
query_used: SparseCheck: Calibrated Retrieval for Low-Resource Scientific QA

paper.title: SparseCheck: Calibrated Retrieval for Low-Resource Scientific QA
paper.tldr: SparseCheck combines sparse retrieval, calibration, and reranking for low-resource scientific question answering.

abstract: SparseCheck improves retrieval-augmented scientific question answering on low-resource domains by using sparse lexical retrieval, a calibration head, and a lightweight reranker. The paper reports a 78.4 macro-F1 score on SciQA-LongTail and states that all experiments can be reproduced from the released code and public datasets.

paper_claims.code_availability: The official implementation is available at https://github.com/sparsecheck/sparsecheck and includes preprocessing, training, and evaluation scripts.
paper_claims.dataset_availability: All SciQA-LongTail train, validation, and benchmark holdout splits are public and require no approval.
paper_claims.primary_metric: The primary metric is macro-F1 on the benchmark holdout split.
paper_claims.reported_result: SparseCheck reaches 78.4 macro-F1 on SciQA-LongTail holdout.
paper_claims.compute_claim: The full reproduction can be completed on a single 24GB GPU in under 12 hours.
paper_claims.baseline_claim: The paper compares against BM25, Contriever, and a dense reranker baseline using the same data splits.

limitations: The paper does not provide full ablation logs.
The paper does not describe dataset application requirements because it states the dataset is fully public.
